author: y4rr
content:
Filed while closing #379. The auto-instrumentation infrastructure landed in ferrotorch-core::profiler_hook with a representative set of ops instrumented (add, sub, mul, matmul, sum, softmax). The pattern for adding more ops is mechanical:

1. Rename the existing top-level function body to a private inner helper, e.g. `add_inner`
2. Replace the body with: `profile_op_scope("<op_name>", "<category>", &[input.shape(), ...], || op_inner(...))`
3. Keep device-mismatch / shape-validation errors OUTSIDE the scope so error paths aren't timed

Categories used so far:
- "tensor_op" — add/sub/mul/div/neg/abs/pow/sqrt/etc. (elementwise math)
- "linalg" — matmul/mm/bmm/dot/cholesky/inv/etc.
- "reduction" — sum/mean/prod/sum_dim/mean_dim/logsumexp/etc.
- "activation" — relu/gelu/silu/sigmoid/tanh/softmax/log_softmax
- "shape" — reshape/view/permute/narrow/transpose (currently uninstrumented since they're zero-copy and timing them is mostly noise — but adding them would let users see the shape transitions in trace output)
- "conv" — conv1d/2d/3d/conv_transpose
- "norm" — batch_norm/layer_norm/group_norm/rms_norm
- "loss" — cross_entropy/mse/bce/etc.

Group by file to avoid touching too many at once:
- ferrotorch-core/src/grad_fns/arithmetic.rs — div, neg, pow, sqrt, abs (the rest of the elementwise family)
- ferrotorch-core/src/grad_fns/transcendental.rs — exp, log, sin, cos, etc.
- ferrotorch-core/src/grad_fns/reduction.rs — mean, prod, sum_dim, mean_dim, logsumexp, logsumexp_dim
- ferrotorch-core/src/grad_fns/activation.rs — relu, gelu, silu, etc.
- ferrotorch-core/src/grad_fns/linalg.rs — bmm_differentiable, matmul_differentiable
- ferrotorch-core/src/grad_fns/shape.rs — reshape, expand, cat, stack (the ones with grad_fn nodes)
- ferrotorch-nn — Linear, Conv2d, BatchNorm, LayerNorm, MultiheadAttention, etc.

Each file is ~10-30 ops; expect ~5 lines of change per op.
